description: User can open a browser dashboard that auto-detects the viewer timezone and shows a live local Claude promotion clock.
verify: Run the app
Open the dashboard in a browser
Confirm the timezone label matches the browser timezone
Confirm the live clock and countdown update without a refresh

description: User can see an infographic of historical peak and off-peak windows converted into the viewer timezone.
verify: Open the dashboard
Inspect the weekly matrix and history timeline
Confirm each window is labeled as peak or off-peak and shown in local time

description: User can see a forecasted next promotion campaign plus next likely off-peak and peak transitions computed from official published campaign history rather than hard-coded.
verify: Open the dashboard
Inspect the forecast card
Confirm the estimate includes next off-peak and next peak timestamps, confidence scores, and supporting rationale

description: The backend can discover official Claude promotion articles from the web and normalize them into campaign and window records without relying on local files.
verify: Start the API
Request /api/promotions
Confirm the response includes official source URLs, campaign records, and expanded windows

description: The React app follows the Figma-derived reference implementation while treating refs/design as a non-runtime reference bundle.
verify: Compare src/app and src/styles against refs/design
Confirm the runtime app does not import from refs/design

description: User can distinguish official live promotion status from historical or inferred pressure patterns, so the clock does not overstate certainty when no promotion is live.
verify: Open the dashboard outside an official promotion window
Confirm the clock says no promotion is live
Confirm the historical pattern is shown separately as an inference aid

description: Backend continues serving campaign and phase forecasts if holiday context data is unavailable.
verify: Simulate a holiday API failure
Request the promotion snapshot
Confirm the response still includes campaign, next off-peak, and next peak forecasts

description: Developer can generate repeatable Playwright audit screenshots and a runtime log for the live app.
verify: Start the app locally
Run npm run audit:screenshots
Confirm desktop and mobile screenshots plus a JSON audit log are produced

description: GitHub validates the app build and backend promotions API automatically on push and pull request.
verify: Push the repository to GitHub
Inspect the CI workflow run
Confirm it installs dependencies, runs npm run check, and smoke-tests /api/promotions

description: A Netlify production deploy serves the frontend and the promotions backend from the same domain so /api/promotions does not 404 in production.
verify: Deploy the current repository to Netlify
Request /api/promotions from the Netlify domain
Confirm the dashboard loads live promotion data instead of showing a 404 API error
